---
pagetitle: "fmrimod"
toc: false
sidebar: false
page-layout: full
jupyter: python3
---

::: {.hero}
# fmrimod

[fMRI signal modeling, from HRF specification to design matrices and regression.]{.lead}

[Get started](get-started.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
[Browse the API](reference/index.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-secondary}
:::

## Why fmrimod?

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### Unified
Build HRFs, event terms, design matrices, contrasts, GLMs, and single-trial models from one Python package.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### Familiar
Use NumPy and pandas inputs directly while keeping R-style fMRI formula workflows available.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### Verifiable
R parity fixtures and focused numerical tests keep design matrices, HRFs, and regression contracts explicit.
:::
:::

:::

## In 30 Seconds

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import fmrimod as fm

events = pd.DataFrame({
    "onset": [0, 12, 24, 36],
    "condition": ["face", "scene", "face", "scene"],
})

sf = fm.SamplingFrame(blocklens=40, TR=1.0)
model = fm.event_model(
    'onset ~ hrf(condition, basis="spmg1")',
    data=events,
    sampling_frame=sf,
)

model.design_matrix.shape
```

[Read the full tutorial](get-started.qmd)
